name: Submit — out-of-range input (score > 10) is rejected before proof gen

planSteps.description: Dismiss the onboarding modal if present
Enter 15 in the baseline severity input (outside the valid 1-10 range that the Noir circuit enforces)
Enter 3 in the outcome severity input
Attempt to click the 'Generate Proof' button

The app rejects the out-of-range input BEFORE attempting proof generation. It should either (a) clamp the input to the valid range with a visible indication, (b) show an inline validation error like 'Value must be between 1 and 10', or (c) disable the Generate Proof button. It must NOT let the user submit an invalid input and then fail cryptically at the circuit-execution step, since Noir will produce an unhelpful low-level error for out-of-range inputs.
